residuals.guard: native audit output pipes were not created
residuals.rationale: subprocess.PIPE makes both streams non-null under the supported Python contract; replacing Popen to manufacture a null stream would test a simulation rather than the public adapter boundary, so the fail-closed guard remains explicit and expiring
revisit_triggers: a supported Python runtime permits null PIPE streams
the adapter process transport changes

residuals.guard: Bubblewrap provenance changed during native audit execution
residuals.rationale: honest activation requires replacing the root-owned installed Bubblewrap package during a live audit; CI must not mutate that host trust anchor, and a patched provenance function would not exercise the operating-system boundary
revisit_triggers: a disposable package-manager integration host becomes available
sandbox provenance moves to a repository-owned executable

residuals.guard: was not created as a single-link regular file
residuals.rationale: O_CREAT plus O_EXCL creates a new regular file and the retained descriptor is validated before transfer; manufacturing a non-regular or already-multilink result would require replacing kernel open/fstat behavior rather than exercising the public bootstrap boundary
revisit_triggers: bootstrap creation moves to a non-regular storage primitive
the supported kernel changes O_CREAT plus O_EXCL semantics

residuals.rationale: the guard covers another process creating the exact leaf after the public precheck but before descriptor-relative O_EXCL; deterministic tests already replace parents, files, Git ownership, and ignored state, while forcing this sub-instruction interval would add scheduler probability rather than stronger no-overwrite evidence
revisit_triggers: a deterministic kernel-assisted leaf-create handshake is available
bootstrap no longer uses descriptor-relative O_EXCL

residuals.guard: repository root identity changed during Git checks
residuals.rationale: real-process tests replace the complete repository root immediately after policy creation and prove rollback; hitting the earlier interval inside fixed-root Git plumbing would require a probabilistic scheduler race or patched syscall result and would not strengthen the retained-descriptor guarantee
revisit_triggers: a deterministic Git-command boundary mutation hook becomes public
bootstrap Git checks move behind a kernel transaction

residuals.guard: created bootstrap path is no longer a single-link regular file
residuals.rationale: deterministic inotify tests replace both created leaves with real regular files and exercise identity mismatch plus safe rollback; changing a leaf into a symlink or multi-link file inside the narrower stat interval would be scheduler-dependent and supplies no stronger result than the existing real replacement proof
revisit_triggers: a deterministic post-create hardlink mutation harness is available
bootstrap leaf validation changes

residuals.rationale: supported POSIX hosts provide O_NOFOLLOW and O_DIRECTORY; deleting os constants in-process would simulate an unsupported platform instead of validating the real descriptor-bound public bootstrap
revisit_triggers: a non-POSIX bootstrap backend is implemented
the supported operating-system contract changes

residuals.rationale: real-process tests prove rollback removes exact created identities and preserves concurrently replaced leaves; deleting the leaf inside the final stat-to-unlink interval is harmless and requires a probabilistic scheduler race to observe
revisit_triggers: a deterministic unlink-boundary notification becomes available
bootstrap rollback ownership changes

residuals.guard: platform cannot execute a pinned Git descriptor
residuals.rationale: supported Linux runners expose procfs or devfs descriptor execution; hiding both roots through a patched pathlib method would bypass the platform rather than verify the public Git runner
revisit_triggers: a supported runner lacks both descriptor roots
portable descriptor execution support is added

residuals.guard: trusted Git executable changed while hashing
residuals.rationale: the branch detects a metadata change between two fstat calls on one retained descriptor; deterministic activation requires kernel-scheduled mutation inside that exact interval, while patched fstat results would only simulate the guard
revisit_triggers: a deterministic filesystem-race harness is introduced
executable hashing moves to a kernel-attested primitive

residuals.guard: platform lacks component-safe no-follow file opening
residuals.rationale: the supported POSIX runtime provides O_NOFOLLOW and O_DIRECTORY; deleting os constants in-process would not represent a real supported platform and would conceal the need for native platform CI
revisit_triggers: non-POSIX Git execution becomes supported
the minimum Python or operating-system contract changes

residuals.guard: trusted Git executable changed before descriptor pinning
residuals.rationale: the branch covers replacement after the pre-call snapshot but before descriptor identity comparison; public tests exercise replacements on both sides of execution, while patching open or fstat would not prove the race boundary
revisit_triggers: Git execution no longer uses a retained descriptor
a deterministic filesystem-race harness is introduced

residuals.rationale: the supported Linux runtime mounts procfs for descriptor execution; patching Path.is_dir would simulate an unsupported host rather than exercise descriptor-pinned process creation
revisit_triggers: a supported Linux runner omits procfs
trusted execution gains a second native descriptor path

residuals.rationale: supported hosts provide descriptor-relative open, O_NOFOLLOW, and O_DIRECTORY; deleting those primitives in-process is a forbidden platform simulation rather than native compatibility evidence
revisit_triggers: non-Linux native adapters become supported
the operating-system primitive contract changes

residuals.guard: trusted executable output pipes were not created
residuals.rationale: subprocess.PIPE makes both streams non-null under the supported Python contract; monkeypatching Popen to return an invalid process shape would not exercise the real descriptor-pinned command boundary
revisit_triggers: a supported Python runtime permits null PIPE streams
trusted command process transport changes

negative_searches.rationale: claimed security coverage must use public APIs and real processes, filesystems, or platform CI; production-private calls and monkeypatched internals are prohibited
